heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 21 December 2025 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (191°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: A significant windy period is on-going and is ranked 3rd for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 4 consecutive days beginning 23 December 2025.  The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 3.0°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 563 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 5.4°C), outside temperature (up 4.4°C), cloud levels (down 205 m), and outside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 562.5m lower than yesterday, 205.5m higher than the past week, 387.5m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1349m to a maximum of 3070m, averaging 1286.7m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1081.2m and 899.2m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; extremely cold temperatures will prevail. Also, low humidity will be noticeable and light winds will prevail. Chance of light rain, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and weak sunlight because of clouds. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (2.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
